label: test/project.mnemo file size
notes: Was ~844 KB at 28 memories; grew via append-only history after benchmark writes. compact would collapse old snapshot pages.

label: Effective on-disk cost per memory
notes: Worst case — raw payload is ~50 KB total (~1.6 KB/memory); fixed 512 KiB WAL reservation + page rounding + snapshot history dominate at small N.

label: Logical text+vectors as fraction of file
notes: ~50 KB payload (6.8 KB text + 47.6 KB f32 vectors) inside ~844 KB at 28 memories.

label: Reserved WAL region as fraction of file
notes: 64 pages = 512 KiB reservation, fixed today. Smaller initial WAL would need a config change. WAL share shrinks as N grows.

label: Data/catalog/manifest pages as fraction of file
notes: 38 pages = 311 KB of records, catalog snapshots, and history (append-only).

label: AES-256-GCM per-page overhead
notes: 28 bytes/page: 12-byte nonce + 16-byte GCM tag. Encryption is not the source of size bloat at small N.

label: Argon2id KEK derivation on every open
notes: Range observed ~250–300 ms. This cost dominates every sub-second CLI command. Long-lived processes amortize it across many calls.

label: CLI `mnemo info` median latency
notes: KDF-bound: ~275 ms Argon2id + decrypt header/catalog. Release build would be faster on the non-KDF part only.

label: CLI `mnemo list` median latency (full live set)
notes: KDF + decrypt every record; ~90 memories/s decrypt+decode throughput after Argon2id is paid.

label: CLI `mnemo get` median latency (single record)
notes: Per-record decrypt is microseconds; ~290 ms is almost all Argon2id. Point reads at ~3/s if you reopen each time.

label: CLI `mnemo verify` median latency
notes: Same shape as list: KDF + decrypt/validate every live record.

label: Python open + recall top-10 (release wheel)
notes: Same crypto as the CLI but on a release build, and the binary cost is much lower. Long-lived processes that keep the DB open avoid the KDF tax entirely on subsequent calls.

label: Python remember + flush (one durable write)
notes: Includes one MiniLM embedding + one WAL-fsynced transaction. Each durable write costs roughly one commit; append-only layout adds catalog/manifest pages per flush.
